claims.claim: The scenario uses a sandboxed fidelity tier (e.g. container) since it must assert egress to api.weather.example.com
The scenario pins determinism with on_unanswered: fail (and scripted when_question/choose answers only if the skill raises gates)
Content assertions (e.g. transcript_matches/result) are kept in separate assert items from filesystem/egress assertions
Content assertions like transcript_matches/result run on the token-free replay PR gate

egress_* assertions are live-only and never evaluate on replay (they belong on a live/nightly lane), while filesystem keys like file_exists/user_visible_artifact are replay-checkable only when the cassette carries a record-time artifacts manifest
A single assert item should not mix content keys with filesystem/egress keys
A choose: rule that matched the gate but named a label the gate never offered is treated as an authoring bug and hard-fails
This is distinct from an unanswered gate, so on_unanswered: first (or llm) does not backstop a mismatched choose label
The error now prints the offered options plus a closest-match suggestion so the anchor can be fixed from the error alone
A header-only gate (empty question) can never be keyed because the steered answer can't be serialized to it
Every gate must have a non-empty question to be steerable
Recommends verify-run answer-coverage against a kept run to catch these issues in about a second before a paid record
This is intentional behavior, not a bug
The 'first' keyword shorthand is disabled for external --decider-cmd / --decider-dir helpers
The 'first' shorthand stays active only for the built-in --on-unanswered first path
A helper returning the literal string 'first' must match an actual option label named 'first', or the gate fails loud
Rationale: this prevents a helper bug from accidentally emitting 'first' and silently green-ing option 1
Recommends helpers return a real label name or a numeric option index
An egress/boundary test requires a sandboxed tier; container (the default) provides real default-deny egress
protocol (L0) is REJECTED / fails loud (not silently passed) when a scenario asserts egress
microvm's egress transport is the same allowlist proxy as container — it is not better network fidelity
The fidelity tier is set in the scenario fidelity: field, not via a --fidelity flag
A single assert item with more than one key is an AND — it passes only if every key passes
A mixed content+live item (like tool_called + egress_denied) loses its filesystem/egress half on the token-free replay lane
tool_called does not mean 'attempted': tool counts are authoritative and de-duped
A tool that was requested then denied does not register as tool_called
tool_called proves the tool actually ran, not merely that it was attempted
subagent_dispatched matches by the dispatch description too, not only by subagent_type
Skills often dispatch with no subagent_type (agentType is 'unknown'), so matching should use the description
subagent_declared_but_unused fires when a declared sub-agent tool was not used, even if other tools were used
dispatch_count_max is an author-chosen budget / post-hoc count assertion, not a reproduction of a production cap
Cowork imposes no in-conversation Task-dispatch cap to reproduce
dispatch_count_max should be used only to catch a fan-out you don't want
A mode: r mount gets a real :ro bind, so a write fails in-guest
mode: rw vs rwd (delete protection on outputs/ or connected folders) is NOT mount-enforced — an rm succeeds in-guest
rwd delete-protection is only caught post-hoc by the no_delete_in_outputs assertion
A mode: r folder's contents are recorded body-less (path plus hash, truncated, no body) since it's an input, not a deliverable
file_exists (and computer_links_resolve) still pass on replay against the hash-only placeholder
artifact_json reports evidence-unavailable on every lane (live, verify-run, replay) for a mode: r file
questions_count_max counts sub-questions, not gates — one AskUserQuestion call can bundle several sub-questions
A single gate bundling 3 sub-questions counts as 3 against questions_count_max
Recommends reading the per-gate sub-question count from trace --view questions
gate_answers_delivered: true passes vacuously when no gate fires at all
Whether a gate fires is model-dependent, so gate_answers_delivered can't catch 'the gate never fired'
Pair gate_answers_delivered with gate_answer_count_min: 1 when the presence of a gate matters
prompt_asset_missing is a WARN-severity verdict signal, not a hard failure
The run proceeded and can still green even though a prompt asset referenced by the scenario was not found
A missing prompt asset means the model ran against an incomplete prompt
Advises scanning the verdict signals array after every run
Recommends treating prompt_asset_missing as a blocking error in CI by checking the signals array
A green run with a missing prompt asset is not a valid pass — fix the asset path
Keep .env OUT of any mounted folder — a mounted folder is copied into the sandbox and the token could leak
.env resolution order is: env vars, then --dotenv, then ./.env, then the install .env
A git worktree's ./.env is gitignored/absent, causing 'no model credentials' — fix with --dotenv <main-checkout>/.env
record detects a secret embedded in a base64 artifact and replaces the whole body with a [REDACTED:base64] marker plus a warning
An artifact_json/content assertion targeting that artifact fails at replay because the body no longer matches
Fix: don't let secrets flow into artifacts; for intentionally opaque artifacts, drop the content assertion and gate on file_exists on the live lane
file_exists is satisfied by the path being present in the recorded artifacts manifest, not by the file size or content, so a 0-byte or placeholder outputs/report.md passes exactly like a real one

Whether file_exists is even evaluated on replay depends on the cassette carrying a record-time artifacts manifest (with one it runs against the manifest; a manifest-less cassette skips it rather than failing)

The fix is to assert on content rather than mere existence: a content-class assertion (e.g. transcript_contains or transcript_matches on a stable marker) that survives replay and fails on empty output
Recommends scripted answers: a when_question regex matched to a choose rule in the scenario's answers: block
Recommends pairing scripted answers with on_unanswered: fail for deterministic CI
States that llm is not a valid --on-unanswered CLI value; use --decider-llm or on_unanswered: llm in the YAML instead
Both --decider-llm and on_unanswered: llm are flagged as nonDeterministic
--on-unanswered first is also flagged nonDeterministic, so it is not a deterministic substitute
Concludes scripted answers are the correct deterministic path for CI
result: success only means the agent didn't error — it does not mean the task was completed
A turn ending with a plain-text re-ask after an already-answered gate still reports result: success
The stalled verdict signal is designed to catch a run ending on a question with no productive tool work after its last gate
The stalled signal is a tool-position heuristic: any post-gate tool call, even an errored one, clears the flag (false negative)
A deliverable written before a final confirmation gate does not clear the stalled flag (false positive) — use allow_stall: true there
Recommends asserting the actual deliverable (file_exists/artifact_json/transcript_matches) and checking the verdict signals array for stalled, rather than relying on result: success alone
web_fetch is gated by URL provenance (whether the URL appeared in the prompt or a prior web_fetch result), not by the egress hostname allowlist
Adding a host to egress.extra_allow is a no-op here because the approval gate is orthogonal to the allowlist
Fix 1: put the URL directly in the prompt, making it provenanced and skipping the gate (and the allowlist) entirely
Fix 2: answer the webfetch:<domain> approval gate deterministically via a scripted when_tool rule with grant: domain|once, or a session web_fetch.approved_domains entry
The webfetch approval gate is fail-closed — it is not auto-allowed, and --on-unanswered first does not grant it
No blanket re-record needed: replay --assert-from <scenario.yaml> re-evaluates the edited assert: block against each committed cassette token-free
questions_count_max is a gate-lifecycle key that needs the cassette's controlOut stream; any modern recording already has it
Only a pre-controlOut cassette needs a single re-record to permanently unlock gate assertions
--assert-from hard-fails if the scenario's answers: differ from the recording's answers, so assert-only edits must be sequenced separately from answer edits
Answer edits (unlike assert-only edits) do force a re-record
Should not recommend re-recording the whole fleet or hand-editing the cassettes' frozen assert blocks
Reads the cassette top-level effectiveFidelity field (hostloop in this fixture) as the tier the live run actually resolved to
Explains fidelity: cowork means resolve from the baseline's host-loop gate at record time, so the authored value alone does not tell you the tier
Points to verify-cassettes to catch future drift: it raises a resolved-tier staleness finding when a cowork cassette effectiveFidelity no longer matches what the current baseline resolves to
Mentions unverifiable-tier for a cassette that predates effectiveFidelity
Does not incorrectly claim the scenario fidelity: field answers the question
The file format is plain text: one regex pattern per line
Blank lines and lines starting with # are ignored
Patterns are matched against the WHOLE finding token (anchored, not substring), and matching is case-sensitive unless the regex carries an i flag
File entries are ALL-CLASS (equivalent to bare --allow), unlike a class-scoped flag like --allow-domain
Moving a class-scoped allow into the file widens its scope (e.g. a domain pattern could also suppress a path/email finding)
--allow-patterns-file takes a path to a file OF patterns, not a path to allow directly
Each cassette is written via a same-dir temp file + atomic rename, so a crash can't leave a partial/corrupt cassette
Scenarios in a batch are independent; one failure does not roll back or halt siblings
The batch continues on a failure but exits non-zero (1) if any scenario failed, so CI still fails loud
Runs are fully isolated per scenario (unique sidecar networks/proxy), so --concurrency doesn't cause cross-talk
The batch is NOT all-or-nothing/transactional, and a mid-batch failure does not corrupt previously written cassettes
